content.problemStatement:
Sidaga calls a sequence beautiful if the sum of elements of this sequence is strictly positive.

Kaspers gave Sidaga a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and asked him to partition it into exactly KKK non-empty **contiguous** subsequences such that each element of AAA belongs to exactly one subsequence and all KKK subsequences are beautiful.

Sidaga is getting ready for his wedding - he is busy learning how to dance, so he cannot solve this problem. Can you help him?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to partition AAA into KKK contiguous subsequences or `"NO"` otherwise.

If it is possible to partition AAA, print a second line containing KKK space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BKB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_KB1​,B2​,…,BK​ denoting the sizes of the subsequences, where 1≤Bi1 \le B_i1≤Bi​ for each valid iii and B1+B2+…+BK=NB_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_K = NB1​+B2​+…+BK​=N. The first B1B_1B1​ elements of AAA belong to the first subsequence, the next B2B_2B2​ elements belong to the second subsequence and so on.

If there is more than one solution, you may print any one.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:
In first case, there are more than one options. Another such valid option is {2,2}.
In second case, it is not possible.
